epics.title: Replace the design-rule-engine aesthetic profile model

tasks.title: Synthesize public design-skill references into profile requirements
acceptance_criteria: The run artifacts identify Anthropic frontend-design, OpenAI frontend-skill, Impeccable, and UI UX Pro Max as external references only.
The selected profile model distinguishes product surfaces from brand/editorial surfaces instead of using the old warm restrained candidate set.
The plan keeps Anthropic public skills as references unless installed in the active Codex skill registry.
done_definition: External references are captured in design constraints and build notes.
No external skill installation is performed.

tasks.title: Rewrite aesthetic profile candidates and anti-patterns
acceptance_criteria: specs/design-rule-engine/aesthetic-profile.json no longer selects or lists the old warm restrained profile set.
The selected profile is product-first and suitable for repository app/workflow surfaces.
Candidate profiles cover product/workflow, brand/editorial, data-dense, and music/visual-led surfaces.
Anti-patterns and open questions match the new profile model.
done_definition: The selected profile and candidates are internally consistent.

tasks.title: Align related design-rule-engine wording with the replacement profile
acceptance_criteria: Stable principles and reference-stack wording no longer claim warm restrained editorial utility as the default profile.
Review rubric aesthetic prompts no longer hard-code the discarded profile.
Runtime token files remain unchanged unless a schema or reference conflict requires a documentation-only note.
done_definition: Related JSON and Markdown files remain parseable.
No rendered UI or runtime token source is changed.

tasks.title: Validate AI-team artifacts and changed design-rule-engine JSON
acceptance_criteria: Changed design-rule-engine JSON files are parsed successfully.
Verification records that browser checks are skipped because no rendered UI changed.
done_definition: Verifier artifact records pass/fail status and any skipped visual checks.
Design and code reviews approve the documentation/data-only change or record follow-up risks.

assumptions: The user wants to discard the current candidate profiles, not the entire design-rule-engine directory.
The replacement should not install or vendor external skills in this task.
No runtime CSS token changes are desired until the user approves a visual direction and browser verification task.
risks: Changing profile language without changing runtime tokens can create a temporary mismatch between design intent and CSS variables.
External skill sources are evolving; their ideas should be treated as reference guidance rather than frozen product requirements.
A product-first selected profile may need later adjustment if the repository's primary surface becomes brand or marketing oriented.
